Vector Search and Embeddings

Vector search uses dense embeddings to find semantically similar documents. Unlike keyword matching, it captures semantic meaning.

How It Works

Documents are converted into high-dimensional vectors using embedding models like sentence-transformers. Queries are embedded the same way.

Approximate Nearest Neighbors

FAISS and Milvus are popular libraries for approximate nearest neighbor search. They trade a small accuracy loss for massive speed gains.

Hybrid Retrieval

Combining BM25 lexical scores with vector semantic scores often produces better results than either method alone. Reciprocal Rank Fusion is one way to combine them.
